<12>CEF:0|NetScout Systems|Omnis Cyber Intelligence|6.3.5|Threat Intelligence|4.1.8.2|7|type=1 desc=Malicious IP/Url:4.1.8.2( Policy:CnC Other; Category:Other Command and Control,T1071-Application Layer Protocol,T1437-Application Layer Protocol,Mobile CnC,Spyware,T1041-Exfiltration Over C2 Channel; Classification:TA0011-Command and Control,Malware,TA0037-Command and Control,Mobile,Command and Control,TA0010-Exfiltration) encountered for 2 hosts in last 60 minutes  iocDescription=(CnC Other,A Botnet Command and Control alert is triggered when traffic associated with botnet command and control activity has been observed. Bots are the individual nodes in a botnet,and are most often coordinated with a central Command&Control point (C&C).   A bot infection often means that an attacker has full control over the infected system and therefore a variety of malicious activities and threats to security are present.A huge variety of bot malware exists, therefore analysis of potentially compromised machines and network activity is suggested. If compromise activity is found, useful indicators should be leveraged to discover other compromised systems that may be associated with any given attack campaign in question.) mitreTactic=TA0011-Command and Control mitreTechnique=T1071-Application Layer Protocol iocConfidence=100 srcCount=1 dstCount=1 srcHostGroupCount=1 dstHostGroupCount=1 app=IP_MAL_TRF interfaceCount=2 violationCount=2 start=1707847200000 end=1707850800000 url=https://1.1.1.1:8443/console/?modID=idRiskVisualization&modMsg=params&category=2&ruleId=3016850&start=1707847200000&end=1707850800000
